big CIRCUS WAR'-SUN FEAT


A PICTURESQUE circus war,


~ the like of which has never
- - been seen since the Barnum
& Bailey show declared war on the
Ringing Brothers several years
ago, is now impending, and Spo-
kane is sure to feel the effects of it
this year, according to Harold
Bushea. publicity man of the AL G..
Barnes show, the "made-in-Spo-
kane" circus which will be one of
the big features of carnival week
next month.


Mr.. Bushea. an old man in the
circus business, came to Spokane
a few weeks ago to take up his
present work. He is cooperating
with the merchants and travelling
mems committees which are pro-
noting the big Spokane carnival
and exposition. OF the impending
circus war he said to The Press
last week:


Ringing Brothers and the shows
they control form what might be
termed the circus trust and they
figure on exerting every effort this
year to crush out the Sells-Floto
people, one of the remaining few
big circuses not in the combine.
Spokane is going to notice the gil
gantic fight by the record-breaking
advertising campaign which will be
waged here this summer and by
the contest between the two fac
ton of the circus world to see which
